title: lupinus microcarpus var. recurvatus
intro: Lupinus microcarpus var. recurvatus, also known as the Baja Lupine or Spurred Lupine, is a variety of the annual lupine plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lupinus microcarpus var. recurvatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
